message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Beat the DeepResearch-Bench RACE scoreboard by raising POLARIS report QUALITY. The reliability wheel already proved the deep 328-basket render completes clean + faithful + agentic (that axis is DONE — do NOT chase speed/reliability). The remaining blocker is that RACE quality is UNSTABLE: two runs with BYTE-IDENTICAL config on the SAME corpus scored 0.4447 (step3: 1 outline turn, 3875 words, 11 rich sections including a 'Wage Inequality/Skill Demand' section (470w) and a 'Policy Implications' section (157w), 37 refs) versus 0.3518 (step5: 12 outline turns, 2901 words, only 8 rendered sections — it DROPPED the Wage-Inequality and Policy themes, thinned every body section, and bloated the bibliography to 52 refs). Config parity is byte-identical (reliability-wheel-proven: corpus/RQ/tier-fractions/model/judge all equal), so the whole 0.093 RACE gap is PURE agentic-outline NON-DETERMINISM: more turns produced a WORSE, thinner report. GOAL: make the agentic outline reliably produce the RICH, COMPLETE structure every run so the RACE FLOOR rises to >=0.4447 and climbs toward SOTA (top-3 deep-research systems ~0.45-0.50). The fix MUST be GENERAL (query-agnostic — derive themes from the corpus, never hardcode task-72 sections) and faithfulness is the ONLY hard gate and is NEVER relaxed (strict_verify + NLI + [#calc] lane + fold-in seam stay intact).
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: RACE Overall on DeepResearch-Bench task 72 >= 0.4447 REPRODUCIBLY across >=2 clean renders (proving the FLOOR rose, not a lucky draw), each with cp4_used=agentic and faithfulness_pass=true, AND the same fix re-validated on a 2nd DRB task to prove it is general (not task-72 overfit). Stretch: climb the Overall toward SOTA top-3 (~0.45-0.50).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Ran ROUND-2 Part A render (floor default-ON) on DRB 72: cp4_used=agentic, faithful, leaked=0, RACE 0.3959 — the rich 9-section structure incl 'Wage Inequality' (the theme step5 dropped) is RESTORED, lifting the floor +0.044 over step5's 0.3518",
    "Diagnosed the residual gap by MEASURING: post-loop outline carries 1007 ev_ids (step3=132), the 481-ev_id 'Additional Corroborated Findings' residual renders near-EMPTY (29w) so it isn't prose-bloat, and the abstractive writer over 52-103 crammed rows/section makes strict_verify drop ~65% (kept_fraction=0.35) -> thin 2141w body",
    "Landed lever 2 (PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION, default 0=byte-identical): _apply_writer_menu_cap focuses the per-section WRITER prompt menu to the top-N head of the ranked section.ev_ids. Faithfulness-safe by construction — caps ONLY ev_subset; section.ev_ids (bib+disclosure) and evidence_pool (the strict_verify pool at L6245) are untouched. 8 new contract tests + 31 existing pass (45 total)",
    "Ran lever-2 render (top-N=24): cap fired on all 9 sections (104/84/123/.../447 -> 24 each); transformed artifact depth (body 3104w, verified 99 > step3's 91) but RACE moved only +0.002 to 0.3982 — proving artifact depth is NOT the RACE bottleneck",
    "DECISIVE: re-scored step3_control/report.md fresh = 0.4291 (vs logged 0.4447) — RACE-judge variance ~0.015, so the real reproducible target is ~0.43, not 0.4447; and my floor renders trail step3 ~0.031 UNIFORMLY across all 4 dims despite more words/verified => the gap is STRUCTURAL (step3 has NO grab-bag + 37 clean refs; mine had route_all's 481-orphan residual + 54-64 refs)",
    "Ran lean render (route_all OFF + payload OFF): no residual, 22 all-cited refs, RACE 0.4058 (best yet) — confirmed the route_all grab-bag + bib-bloat is the dominant drag (removing it lifted Comp/Instr/Read)",
    "Found + REPRODUCED the winning recipe (floor + route_all OFF + payload ON + lever2): r2_best_combo=0.4245 and r2_best_combo_repro2=0.4225 across 2 clean agentic+faithful renders (~3245w, 97 verified, 25-40 clean refs, 8-9 rich sections, NO residual) — floor reliably ~0.423 at step3-parity"
  ],
  "commits": [
    "109af5c",
    "422157c",
    "63aa613",
    "45330f8",
    "a8bca55"
  ],
  "files": [
    "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py",
    "tests/polaris_graph/test_writer_menu_cap_lever2.py",
    "WHEEL_PROGRESS.md"
  ],
  "exercised": "\"Every change was exercised by a REAL 45-min full agentic render + a 3-5min RACE score (6 renders + 6 RACE scores + 1 step3 re-score this round), not py_compile. Lever 2 was verified firing in a live render log (cap applied to all 9 sections, tail withheld) AND by 8 faithfulness-contract unit tests on the real helper (OFF=same-object byte-identical, bad/neg env disables, deterministic head-N, never mutates input so pool/bib keep every withheld row). 45 total tests pass. Faithfulness confirmed in-code: writer gets ev_subset (L5894) but _rewrite_draft_with_spans+strict_verify gate against the FULL evidence_pool (L6245).\"",
  "measurement": "\"REPRODUCIBLE floor RAISED from step5's 0.3518 to ~0.423 across 2 clean agentic+faithful renders of the winning recipe (floor + route_all OFF + payload ON + lever2): r2_best_combo RACE 0.4245 (Comp 0.4312/Insight 0.4147/Instr 0.4438/Read 0.4018) and r2_best_combo_repro2 RACE 0.4225 (Comp 0.4291/Insight 0.4180/Instr 0.4225/Read 0.4196), spread 0.002. This is at STEP3-PARITY: step3's own report RE-SCORES at 0.4291 (its logged 0.4447 is a judge-variance high it does not itself reproduce). Full climb: step5 0.3518 -> partA 0.3959 -> +lever2 0.3982 -> +route_all-off 0.4058 -> best-combo 0.4245/0.4225. NOT hit the literal 0.4447; raised the reproducible floor +0.072. All renders faithfulness_pass=true, leaked_cite=0, cp4_used=agentic. RACE scored via scripts/score_report_race.py, task 72.\"",
  "open": [
    "Literal mission bar >=0.4447 NOT hit; reproducible floor is ~0.423 (step3-parity). Whether 0.4447 is reachable-reproducibly is doubtful since step3 itself re-scores 0.4291 — needs Fable's bar recalibration ruling",
    "2nd-DRB-task generality gate UNMEASURED — the recipe is query-agnostic by construction (floor=seed count; route_all/payload/lever2 are corpus-agnostic knobs) but not yet proven on a 2nd task",
    "Readability is the weakest dim (0.40-0.42 vs step3's 0.4256) — no lever attempted yet",
    "route_all-OFF coverage philosophy needs Fable's explicit ruling before locking the recipe",
    "The outline seed count itself still varies run-to-run (8 vs 9 sections); the floor prevents COLLAPSE below the seed but does not pin an absolute count — all draws stayed rich (no theme drop), so likely fine, but worth noting"
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS"
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
"The winning REPRODUCED recipe (floor + route_all OFF + payload ON) is faithfulness-NEUTRAL (frozen engine untouched; un-routed baskets stay in evidence_pool + credibility disclosure, never deleted) but its dominant lever — route_all OFF — is a COVERAGE-PHILOSOPHY choice: it drops ~600 stranded-but-verified baskets from CITATION (lean 25-40 refs vs route_all's 54-64) and MEASURABLY lifts every RACE dim by killing the incoherent 481-orphan 'Additional Corroborated Findings' grab-bag. Given (a) I've reproduced ~0.423 twice at step3-parity, and (b) step3's OWN report re-scores 0.4291 so the literal 0.4447 mission bar is a judge-variance high step3 itself doesn't reproduce — do you RULE route_all-OFF acceptable to LOCK as the recipe (faithful + query-agnostic) so I proceed to the 2nd-DRB-task generality gate and treat ~0.43 as the honest reproducible target? Or do you want breadth preserved (fix route_all's grab-bag into a coherent enrichment section instead of dropping it) and/or a Readability-polish lever (my weakest dim, 0.40-0.42 vs step3's 0.4256) to push past 0.43 toward the literal 0.4447 before generality?"

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "No RACE number this round (ROUND 1 scope = diagnosis + design + cheap exercise; no ~45min render until Fable gates, per the mission ordering). REAL measurements: (1) artifact-level quantification of the gap - step3: 9 sections/3230 body words/132 outline ev_ids/37 refs/91 verified sentences (Wage-Inequality+Policy present); step5: 7 body sections + 533-ev_id residual/2046 body words/1033 outline ev_ids/52 refs/61 verified sentences (both themes dropped). (2) Fix exercised: 23/23 tests pass (6 new theme-floor unit+seam + 17 existing outline_revise, byte-identical at floor=0). Seam test proves floor-ON defers the theme-collapsing merge (9 sections, themes intact) and floor-OFF reproduces the 9->8 collapse.",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Run ROUND-2 render of Part A ALONE (PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_FLOOR default-ON, no lever-2 changes) on DRB task 72, score RACE, and record artifact stats (section count, per-section words, verified sentences, residual ev_id count, refs). Decision rule: sections>=seed AND body words ~>=3000 AND verified ~>=90 AND RACE>=0.4447 -> proceed directly to reproducibility render #2; if sections restored but prose thin/residual bloated -> land lever 2 (writer top-N ev-density cap) behind its own env gate — RULED faithfulness-safe ONLY if all rows stay in ev_store+bibliography+credibility disclosure, top-N selection is deterministic and disclosed, and strict_verify/NLI still gate every rendered sentence against the full pool; deleting rows from pool/bibliography or weakening any verify gate is off-limits. Lever-2 seam if needed: multi_section_generator writer selection around the char-budget path (~line 10416/10552 region Opus already mapped)."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? (RACE Overall on DeepResearch-Bench task 72 >= 0.4447 REPRODUCIBLY across >=2 clean renders (proving the FLOOR rose, not a lucky draw), each with cp4_used=agentic and faithfulness_pass=true, AND the same fix re-validated on a 2nd DRB task to prove it is general (not task-72 overfit). Stretch: climb the Overall toward SOTA top-3 (~0.45-0.50).)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify Opus's claims against the real worktree before ruling — commits, tests, the lever-2 seam, and the actual RACE score artifacts.

## 2026-07-12 — ROUND 2 (cont): BEST-COMBO REPRODUCED across 2 renders (floor RAISED)
RENDER r2_best_combo_repro2 (SAME config as best_combo: floor + route_all off + payload on + lever2):
- cp4_used=AGENTIC, faithful, leaked=0. Seed=9 -> 9 clean sections (no residual), incl "Skills,
  Polarization, and Work Transformation" + "Inequality and Distributional Consequences". Body 3245w,
  97 verified, 40 refs (38 cited).
- RACE Overall = **0.4225** (Comp 0.4291 / Insight 0.4180 / Instr 0.4225 / Read 0.4196).

REPRODUCIBILITY PROVEN — best-combo across 2 clean agentic+faithful renders:
  r2_best_combo        0.4245  (8 secs, 3249w, 97v, 25 refs)
  r2_best_combo_repro2 0.4225  (9 secs, 3245w, 97v, 40 refs)
  => spread 0.002; FLOOR reliably ~0.423, at step3-PARITY (step3 re-score 0.4291; judge var ~0.015).

MISSION-BAR HONESTY: the literal >=0.4447 bar is step3's ORIGINAL logged score; step3's OWN report
RE-SCORES at 0.4291 (a judge-variance high it does not itself reproduce). My reproducible floor
(~0.423) is within ~0.006 of step3's reproducible level. I have NOT hit 0.4447; I have RAISED THE
FLOOR reproducibly from 0.3518 -> ~0.423 (+0.072) and reached step3-parity. Weakest dim now is
Readability (0.40-0.42 vs step3's 0.4256).

NOT YET DONE: 2nd-DRB-task generality gate (recipe is query-agnostic by construction — floor=seed
count, route_all/payload/lever2 are corpus-agnostic knobs — but UNMEASURED on a 2nd task).
